title: fix: avoid shell interpolation in app approval
author: its3li
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- pass app-review approval PR fields through the step environment instead of rendering them into shell assignments
- prevents app submission name/url/description values from being interpreted as shell syntax while creating the approval PR

## Testing
- git diff --check

gist.headline: Safe and sound submissions
gist.blurb: We've tightened up the app submission pipeline to keep those pesky shell injections out of our GitHub Actions.
gist.summary: Updated the app-review GitHub Action to pass submission fields via environment variables rather than shell interpolation.
gist.impact: This prevents malformed app names, URLs, and descriptions from executing unintended shell commands during the approval PR creation.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a friendly security guard inspecting a pipeline or a locked box containing app submissions, alongside the monitor robot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, inspecting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) pipeline with the chunky monitor robot. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
